task_completion.description: Verify that a workspace named 'shp_ne' with the URI 'http://shp_ne.example.org' has been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the GeoServer 'Workspaces' page in the browser showing 'shp_ne' in the list of workspaces, ideally with the specified URI visible.

task_completion.description: Verify that a directory-based store named 'shp_directory' has been created in the 'shp_ne' workspace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the GeoServer 'Stores' page showing 'shp_directory' of type 'Directory of spatial files'. The connection parameters should show it pointing to 'file:///opt/geoserver/data_dir/shp_data/' or similar.

task_completion.description: Verify the countries shapefile is published as a layer with the correct title and EPSG code.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the GeoServer 'Layers' page or layer configuration page showing the name 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries', the title 'Countries (Shapefile)', and the Declared SRS set to 'EPSG:4326'.

task_completion.description: Verify the lakes shapefile is published as a layer with the correct title and EPSG code.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the GeoServer 'Layers' page or layer configuration page showing the name 'ne_110m_lakes', the title 'Lakes (Shapefile)', and the Declared SRS set to 'EPSG:4326'.

task_completion.description: Verify that WMS GetMap images for both layers are saved to the specified paths in the home directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output (like 'ls -l /home/ga/') showing 'countries_map.png' and 'lakes_map.png', or an image viewer/file manager displaying these two files in the /home/ga/ directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the GeoServer web interface to configure the workspace, store, and layers.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating the GeoServer web administration GUI in a browser, rather than just editing XML configuration files in a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually requested the images from the GeoServer WMS endpoint.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for terminal commands (e.g., curl or wget) containing a GeoServer WMS URL with 'request=GetMap' and the correct layer names, proving the images were generated by GeoServer.

integrity.description: Verify the saved PNG files are actual images with content, not empty mock files.
integrity.visual_evidence: File sizes shown in the terminal (via 'ls -lh' or similar) should be greater than 0 bytes (typically several KB or MB), or the screenshots should show the actual map images rendered in a viewer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Natural Earth shapefiles (ne_110m_admin_0_countries and ne_110m_lakes) use the WGS84 geographic coordinate system (EPSG:4326). The datastore type must be 'Directory of spatial files', which is a standard GeoServer feature for publishing shapefile collections.
